title: Written In Butlerï؟½s Sermons
content:
آ آ آ آ Affections, Instincts, Principles, and Powers,
آ آ آ آ Impulse and Reason, Freedom and Control
آ آ آ آ So men, unravelling Godï؟½s harmonious whole.
آ آ آ آ Rend in a thousand shreds this life of ours.
آ آ آ آ Vain labour! Deep and broad, where none may see,
آ آ آ آ Spring the foundations of the shadowy throne
آ آ آ آ Where manï؟½s one Nature, queen-like, sits alone,
آ آ آ آ Centred in a majestic unity;
آ آ آ آ And rays her powers, like sister islands, seen
آ آ آ آ Linking their coral arms under the sea:
آ آ آ آ Or clusterï؟½d peaks, with plunging gulfs between
آ آ آ آ Spannï؟½d by aï؟½rial arches, all of gold;
آ آ آ آ Whereoï؟½er the chariot wheels of Life are rollï؟½d
آ آ آ آ In cloudy circles, to eternity
meaning:
Matthew Arnold's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Written In Butlerï؟½s Sermons"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
